content: Effective AMS interventions: 1) Prospective audit with feedback: pharmacist/ID physician reviews cultures, recommends narrowing, IV-to-PO conversion, dose optimization, duration; 2) Formulary restriction/preauthorization for broad-spectrum agents; 3) Clinical pathways/order sets for common syndromes (CAP, UTI, SSTI); 4) Diagnostic stewardship: appropriate culture collection, procalcitonin algorithms to guide initiation/discontinuation; 5) Time-out at 48-72 hours: reassess need when cultures return; 6) De-escalation: narrow spectrum based on culture results; 7) Duration optimization: shorter courses often equivalent (5 days CAP, 3-5 days uncomplicated cystitis, 7 days Gram-negative bacteremia). Outpatient stewardship addresses ~80% of antibiotic volume: delayed prescribing, symptom management, patient education materials.
